tool.name: sourmash compare
tool.description: Build an N×N similarity matrix from a collection of signatures
tool.command: mkdir -p stage && ## Stage every signature in the list collection under a name keyed by its ## element_identifier so the strain order on the command line is stable and ## the matrix row/col labels (the signature names) line up with the strains. #for $sig in $signatures ln -s '$sig' 'stage/${sig.element_identifier}.sig' && #end for sourmash compare --ksize $ksize --csv '$output' stage/*.sig

param.name: signatures
param.label: Signature list collection
param.help: A 'list' collection of sourmash signature files (e.g. from sourmash sketch, mapped over strains). The element identifier of each element should be the strain name; it is staged as '{strain}.sig'. The signature names become the matrix row/column labels.

param.label: k-mer size
param.help: k-mer size to select from each signature for the comparison (default 31). Must match a k-mer size present in the sketched signatures.

tool.help:
**sourmash compare** — compute an N×N similarity matrix from a set of MinHash signatures.
Given a collection of sourmash signatures (one per strain/genome, typically produced by ``sourmash sketch`` mapped over a list of strains), this tool runs ``sourmash compare`` to estimate the Jaccard similarity between every pair of signatures and writes the resulting symmetric N×N matrix as CSV.
-----
**Input**
- *Signature list collection* — a ``list`` collection of sourmash signature files. Each element's identifier should be the strain name; elements are staged as ``{strain}.sig`` so the command-line order (and therefore the matrix column order) is stable. The matrix row/column **labels** are the signature ``name`` fields, which normally match the strain names.
**Output**
- ``compare.csv`` — an N×N similarity matrix (datatype ``csv``). The first row is the header of strain labels; each subsequent row is one strain's similarities to every strain, with ``1.0`` self-similarity on the diagonal.
-----
**Key options**
- ``--ksize`` k-mer size to select from each signature (default 31). It must match a k-mer size present in the sketched signatures.
-----
**Implementation note**
The wrapper symlinks every element of the list collection into a staging directory as ``{element_identifier}.sig`` and invokes ``sourmash compare --csv '$output' stage/*.sig``.
